description: Schema for the GESP discourse corpus used by LiteratureHero. Each row in the CSV files references a text fragment with GESP stress estimates, doom/agency annotations, and metadata.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Stable unique identifier for the text fragment (e.g., LH-FIC-000001).

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Human-readable source (e.g., novel title, outlet name, platform).

fields.name: source_location
fields.description: Location within the source (chapter/page for books, URL or article ID for news, post ID for social).

fields.name: language
fields.description: ISO language code for the text (e.g., en, fr, es).

fields.name: text
fields.description: Raw text fragment (paragraph, scene, news lede, post body).

fields.name: hex_tag
fields.description: Hex encoding of GESP stress (0xGESP, each nibble 0–F).

fields.name: doom_agency_label
fields.description: Coarse discourse-stress label for doom vs agency.

fields.name: doom_score
fields.description: Optional scalar score for doom intensity.

fields.name: agency_score
fields.description: Optional scalar score for perceived agency.

fields.name: case_family
fields.description: Optional archetype label (e.g., rapid_shock_high_R, elite_implosion, climate_cascade, external_conquest, slow_hollowing, total_collapse).

fields.name: annotator_id
fields.description: Identifier of human annotator or model version that produced the labels.

fields.name: annotation_method
fields.description: Description or code of the annotation protocol (e.g., human_v1, lua_auto_v1, hybrid_v2).

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Timestamp of annotation in ISO 8601 format.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Free-form notes about ambiguity, disagreements, or special context.
